subject: Re: Performance Issue for Exporting Subtomo
body:
Hi Alister，
Thank you for your response. So I have to move my dataset to Warp 2 with a 
better GPU.

It's a sad story that we don't have a GUI Warp supported anymore. In my 
case, there are many misestimated defocus value in my dataset, so adjusting 
CTF fit interactively is really helpful. 

Do you have any plan to introduce a simple GUI back to WARP 2?
Cheers, 
Wang
在2025年4月8日星期二 UTC+8 21:56:15<alist...@gmail.com> 写道：

> Hi Wang,
>
> Warp was originally built with the expectation of having ~16GB GPU memory 
> available, unfortunately your card isn’t big enough and that’s where your 
> issues are coming from.
>
> Please note that desktop warp is unsupported right now, we have a Linux 
> version with a command line interface 
> warpem.com 
> <https://warpem.com/user_guide/warptools/quick_start_warptools_tilt_series/>
>
> <https://warpem.com/user_guide/warptools/quick_start_warptools_tilt_series/> 
> <https://warpem.com/user_guide/warptools/quick_start_warptools_tilt_series/>
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>
> On Apr 8, 2025, at 01:27, Wang GuangLei <a8246...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> ﻿
>
> Hi everyone,
>
> I’m currently using Warp for Windows to export subtomo particles, and I’ve 
> run into a few issues I hope you can help with. The export speed seems 
> unusually slow and varies significantly, ranging from 30 minutes to 16 
> hours for a single tomogram. All of my tomograms have the same size and the 
> same number of particles, so I’m not sure why the exporting time are 
> different.
>
> To provide more context, I’m using a single 1080Ti (11GB) GPU to export 
> 3000 128×128×128 particles from 4096×4096×1000 tomograms in WARP 1.0.9. Is 
> it normal for this process to take over 10 hours to complete?
>
> Additionally, I’ve noticed that some of tomograms may produce  completely 
> black (all pixels are zero) subtomos, and lower exporting speed will 
> produce more black subtomos. This leads me to wonder if this performance is 
> typical for exporting in Warp. At this rate, it looks like I’d need about 
> 60 days to export all of my data.
>
> I also observed the following warnings in the console during the export 
> process:
>
> ```
>
> Received “TomoExport Particles” with 4 arguments, for GPU #0, 10188 MB 
> free.  
>
> ReadDirectory: Warning, in memory: unknown field with tag 65001(0xfde9) 
> encountered.  
>
> ReadDirectory: Warning, in memory: unknown field with tag 65002(0xfdea) 
> encountered.  
>
> ReadDirectory: Warning, in memory: unknown field with tag 65001(0xfde9) 
> encountered.  
>
> ReadDirectory: Warning, in memory: unknown field with tag 65002(0xfdea) 
> encountered.  
>
> ```
>
> Additionally, I’ve noticed that my GPU memory seems to be full, and there 
> is an additional 20GB of shared memory in use. The memory clock is running 
> at 2400 MHz, which is considerably lower than the GPU memory speed. Could 
> this be the case?
>
> Any advice or suggestions would be greatly appreciated! Thanks in advance 
> for your help.
>
> Best wishes
> Wang GuangLei
>
> -- 
> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups 
> "Warp" group.

> .
>
>
